role: Orchestration and service layer for the ArchSpine mirror system, providing all core workflows (sync, check, fix, init, publish, administration) and runtime lifecycle management.
responsibility: Collectively, these services coordinate the complete lifecycle of repository mirroring, validation, and repair: from initialization (scanning, language detection), synchronization (scanning, AST extraction, summarization, commit, reconciliation), validation (check pipeline), automated fixing, LLM configuration management, artifact strategy application, rule template management, execution profile resolution, runtime session management (concurrency, checkpointing, error handling), Claude skill installation, and view rendering for developer insights. They serve as the primary orchestration layer between CLI entry points and engine/infrastructure components, ensuring transactional boundaries, resumability, and structured error handling across all operations.

children.role: Primary orchestration service for the ArchSpine 'check' pipeline — coordinates scanning, AST extraction, validation, and cleanup tasks in a single workflow. Used by CLI commands and programmatic consumers to run governance checks on a repository.
Service-layer data-access function providing ArchSpine dashboard metadata from the .spine control plane.
Service orchestrating the automated fix pipeline for ArchSpine violations.
Service layer function that coordinates file scanning, language detection, and manifest persistence for a given project root.
Orchestration service for persisting and querying LLM configuration settings across global and project scopes, consumed by CLI commands.
Pre-publish validation and safety service for the ArchSpine publish pipeline.

CLI-level orchestration module for applying and verifying the ArchSpine repository artifact strategy (e.g., agent instruction files, gitignore, scripts, rule templates). It coordinates the initial bootstrap and ongoing sync of managed repository files during 'init', 'sync', and 'scan' CLI commands.
Rule template registry providing predefined architectural constraint definitions for the ArchSpine project.
Service-level configuration resolver for execution profiles, mapping resolved LLM settings to a structured generation strategy profile.

Runtime orchestration service that initializes and composes infrastructure dependencies (Config, LLM client, Secrets, view layer) and provides factory methods to create SyncService, CheckService, and FixService instances. Acts as the central dependency container and lifecycle manager for CLI command execution.
Draft interface definition serving as a future contract for runtime service capabilities, intended to centralize access to resolved LLM settings, sync, check, and fix services.
Runtime session orchestrator that manages lifecycle of a single command execution (sync, check, fix) with resumability, protected-output safety, and error handling.
Claude skill lifecycle installer/uninstaller for the ArchSpine project.
Service orchestrator coordinating the multi-stage synchronization pipeline for the ArchSpine mirror system.

Service orchestrator that initializes and configures the complete task execution context, gluing together scanner, aggregator, AST extractor, rule engine, output manager, context engine, and lock manager based on provided options.
Service orchestrating repository landscape analysis by combining scanning, configuration, language discovery, and AST extraction.
This directory implements the view rendering subsystem for ArchSpine, transforming knowledge graph and index data into actionable visual artifacts (Markdown, SVG, JSON) for developer insight.
